refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and site conditions to ensure testability and context-specificity. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct field trials under representative duty cycle and site conditions; collect telemetry data for ≥100 cycles; compute p95 cycle time; compare against CT_target. Verify that safety-critical functions (e.g., load securing, emergency stop) are not bypassed or degraded in pursuit of cycle time.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations within the cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote cycle execution with latency and control responsiveness compatible with cycle time target.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall enforce cycle time constraints without compromising safety-critical interlocks.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry for performance monitoring and KPI validation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle powertrain (engine, transmission, hydraulics) for peak power delivery and cycle speed; implement predictive load management to minimize cycle time variance.
DP-B: Implement hierarchical motion planning with real-time cycle time feedback; use teleoperation to enable operator skill-based optimization of load, travel, and dump phases.
DP-C: Decompose cycle time into phase budgets (load, travel, dump); allocate performance targets to vehicle subsystems and teleoperation latency; enforce via safety-gated performance monitoring.
DP-D: Combine vehicle performance optimization (DP-A) with teleoperation responsiveness (DP-B) and phase-based performance allocation (DP-C) to achieve holistic cycle time control.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Implement an integrated cycle time control architecture combining (1) optimized vehicle powertrain and load management, (2) low-latency teleoperation with operator feedback, and (3) phase-based performance budgeting with safety-gated monitoring and telemetry reporting.

potential_sources: Vehicle powertrain optimization may conflict with safety-critical load securing if not properly sequenced.
Teleoperation latency and vehicle responsiveness must be co-designed to avoid instability or operator over-correction.
Safety interlocks (e.g., load securing verification before travel) may add latency; must be optimized without removal.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is a coupled design that requires tight integration across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety domains. Coupling is acceptable if managed via explicit phase sequencing, latency budgets, and safety-gated performance gates. Recommend detailed interface specification and co-simulation during detailed design.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle kinematic and hydraulic limits under site conditions.
Assumption: Teleoperation network link can support required control latency (typically <200 ms round-trip for heavy equipment).
Assumption: Operator skill and training are sufficient to execute optimized cycle phases consistently.
Risk: Aggressive cycle time optimization may increase wear on powertrain and hydraulics, impacting maintenance costs and availability.
Risk: Safety interlocks may introduce latency that prevents CT_target achievement; requires trade-off analysis.
Risk: Site conditions (terrain, load variability, weather) may cause p95 cycle time to exceed target despite optimization.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-D to explicitly specify (1) phase time budgets for load, travel, dump with safety margins, (2) teleoperation latency budget and network requirements, (3) performance monitoring thresholds and feedback mechanisms, and (4) safety-gated performance gates that prevent cycle time optimization from bypassing critical interlocks.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. At this level, IM assignment for cycle time KPI control encompasses the orchestration of vehicle performance, teleoperation responsiveness, and safety-gated performance monitoring across all subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Cycle time data (phase durations, p95 metrics, performance variance) reported from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to external monitoring/analytics systems for KPI validation and operator feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Cycle task commands (load, travel, dump) received from Site Manager or Tele-operator; includes duty cycle parameters and site condition context.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety-critical alerts (e.g., load securing failure, emergency stop triggered) that may impact cycle time execution; communicated to Safety Manager and operator.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel for teleoperation control commands and telemetry feedback; latency and bandwidth must support cycle time target.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for cycle time KPI control. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within this boundary. Minimal diffusion; cycle time orchestration logic resides at solution level.

Vehicle module owns powertrain optimization and load management (DP-A component). However, cycle time control also depends on teleoperation latency and safety interlocks, which are external to Vehicle. Partial containment; cycle time KPI is a system-level concern, not purely vehicle-level.

Teleoperation module owns operator control responsiveness and network latency management (DP-B component). However, cycle time also depends on vehicle performance and safety constraints. Partial containment; teleoperation is a critical enabler but not the sole owner of cycle time KPI.

Safety Services module enforces safety-gated performance monitoring (DP-C component) and may introduce latency constraints. However, Safety Services does not own vehicle performance or teleoperation responsiveness. Partial containment; safety is a constraint on cycle time, not the primary driver.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Cycle time KPI control is fundamentally a system-level concern that requires orchestration across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate boundary for DP-D implementation, with explicit delegation of sub-DPs to Vehicle (powertrain), Teleoperation (control responsiveness), and Safety Services (performance gating). Cycle time monitoring and telemetry reporting are system-level functions that belong in NGHE Solution.
containment_risks: Risk: If cycle time orchestration logic is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services without clear ownership, performance optimization may be suboptimal or conflicting.
Risk: Telemetry collection and KPI computation must be centralized in NGHE Solution to ensure consistent measurement and reporting; if distributed, p95 calculation may be inconsistent.
Risk: Safety-gated performance gates must be enforced at the system level; if implemented only in Safety Services, vehicle and teleoperation modules may bypass constraints.
Risk: Network latency (Network Link interface) is a critical factor in cycle time; if not explicitly managed at NGHE Solution level, teleoperation responsiveness may degrade unpredictably.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign DP-D implementation to NGHE Solution with explicit sub-allocations: (1) Vehicle module owns powertrain optimization and load management (DP-A), (2) Teleoperation module owns control responsiveness and latency management (DP-B), (3) Safety Services module owns safety-gated performance monitoring (DP-C), (4) NGHE Solution owns phase-based performance budgeting, cycle time orchestration, telemetry collection, and KPI reporting. Define clear interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) to coordinate across modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR-NGHE-002 clearly specifies the cycle time KPI (p95 ≤ CT_target) with measurement and site condition context. Selected DP-D provides a comprehensive solution architecture combining vehicle optimization, teleoperation responsiveness, and safety-gated performance control. DP-D directly addresses the FR and is testable via field trials and telemetry analysis. No fundamental mismatch between FR and DP.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Cycle time orchestration logic may be scattered across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services if sub-allocations are not clearly defined. Mitigation: Establish explicit ownership and interface contracts for each sub-DP.

Diffusion Risk 2: Telemetry collection and KPI computation may be duplicated across modules if not centralized. Mitigation: Designate NGHE Solution as the single owner of telemetry aggregation and p95 calculation.

Diffusion Risk 3: Network latency management may be overlooked if not explicitly assigned to Teleoperation module. Mitigation: Include latency budgeting and monitoring in Teleoperation DP-B specification.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for DP-D. Containment score of 95 reflects that cycle time KPI control is a system-level orchestration concern. Sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution and contribute to the overall DP via well-defined interfaces. Minimal diffusion expected if sub-allocations are clearly specified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-002 is feasible via field trials under representative duty cycle and site conditions. Telemetry interface enables collection of cycle time data for p95 analysis. Safety-gated performance monitoring ensures that safety interlocks are not bypassed. Verification plan should include baseline testing, optimization iterations, and final KPI validation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of DP-D to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-allocations to Vehicle (powertrain), Teleoperation (control responsiveness), and Safety Services (performance gating) are well-defined. Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) provide clear communication channels. Ownership and responsibility boundaries are explicit.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Develop detailed phase time budgets (load, travel, dump) with safety margins and performance targets for each subsystem. Include contingency for site condition variability.
Revision 2: Specify teleoperation latency budget (e.g., <200 ms round-trip) and network link requirements (bandwidth, reliability) to support cycle time target.
Revision 3: Define safety-gated performance gates that prevent cycle time optimization from bypassing critical interlocks (e.g., load securing verification, emergency stop responsiveness).
Revision 4: Establish telemetry schema for cycle time data collection, including phase durations, p95 metrics, performance variance, and site condition context.
Revision 5: Plan co-simulation and integration testing to validate coupling between vehicle powertrain, teleoperation latency, and safety constraints.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-002 is a system-level KPI requirement that has been successfully refined and allocated to NGHE Solution with a comprehensive DP-D architecture. However, the requirement is not atomic and has been decomposed into four sub-FRs (vehicle execution, teleoperation responsiveness, safety-gated control, and telemetry reporting). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to the detailed design level. Proceeding with ZIGZAG_DOWN to decompose sub-FRs and allocate them to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations within the cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote cycle execution with latency and control responsiveness compatible with cycle time target.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall enforce cycle time constraints without compromising safety-critical interlocks.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry for performance monitoring and KPI validation.

assumptions: Assumption 1: CT_target is achievable within vehicle kinematic and hydraulic limits under site conditions. Requires validation during detailed design and field trials.
Assumption 2: Teleoperation network link can support required control latency (<200 ms round-trip). Requires network architecture specification and latency testing.
Assumption 3: Operator skill and training are sufficient to execute optimized cycle phases consistently. Requires operator training plan and competency assessment.
Assumption 4: Safety interlocks can be optimized to minimize latency without compromising safety. Requires safety-critical design review and trade-off analysis.
Assumption 5: Site conditions (terrain, load variability, weather) are within the agreed duty cycle envelope. Requires site characterization and duty cycle specification.
open_risks: Risk 1: Aggressive cycle time optimization may increase wear on powertrain and hydraulics, impacting maintenance costs and availability. Mitigation: Conduct durability analysis and establish maintenance intervals.
Risk 2: Safety interlocks may introduce latency that prevents CT_target achievement. Mitigation: Perform safety-critical design review and latency trade-off analysis.
Risk 3: Site conditions may cause p95 cycle time to exceed target despite optimization. Mitigation: Establish contingency plans and performance monitoring thresholds.
Risk 4: Teleoperation network link may experience latency spikes or packet loss under field conditions. Mitigation: Implement network redundancy and latency compensation mechanisms.
Risk 5: Operator fatigue or skill variability may impact cycle time consistency. Mitigation: Develop operator training program and fatigue management protocols.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Detailed phase time budgets (load, travel, dump) with safety margins and performance targets for each subsystem.
Evidence 2: Teleoperation latency budget specification and network link requirements (bandwidth, reliability, latency).
Evidence 3: Safety-critical design review confirming that performance optimization does not bypass critical interlocks.
Evidence 4: Telemetry schema and data collection plan for cycle time monitoring and KPI validation.
Evidence 5: Co-simulation and integration test results validating coupling between vehicle powertrain, teleoperation latency, and safety constraints.
Evidence 6: Field trial data (≥100 cycles) demonstrating p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under representative duty cycle and site conditions.
Evidence 7: Durability analysis and maintenance interval recommendations for optimized powertrain operation.
Evidence 8: Operator training plan and competency assessment for cycle time optimization execution.
